items.mistake: Fitting k-means on raw unscaled features
items.fix: Standardize or normalize numeric features (or document a deliberate scale) before computing Euclidean distances.
items.why: Distance is dominated by columns with larger numeric ranges; clusters become scale artifacts, not meaningful segments.
items.example: Features [age, annual_income] without scaling splits almost entirely on income bins because income values dwarf age.

items.mistake: Picking k from a single inertia elbow without validation
items.fix: Combine elbow/silhouette with cluster profiles, stability across seeds, and business interpretability before committing to k.
items.why: WCSS always decreases as k increases; internal metrics alone cannot prove actionable structure.
items.example: Elbow at k=6 may silhouette-peak at k=4 while k=3 matches known product tiers—prefer the actionable partition.

items.mistake: Running k-means once with one random seed
items.fix: Use `n_init` (multiple restarts), k-means++ initialization, and compare WCSS or assignments across runs.
items.why: k-means converges to local minima; a single bad initialization can hide a better partition.
items.example: `KMeans(n_clusters=5, n_init=10, random_state=0)` tries 10 seeds and keeps the lowest-inertia result.
